key_points:
### Key Compliance Points — Resolution of Stressed Assets (Amendment) Directions, 2026
*(RBI/2026-27/72, dated 2026-04-29; effective 2026-07-01)*

Creates a dedicated framework for resolving loans of borrowers hit by **natural calamities** (RBI/2026-27/72, 2026-04-29).

- **New definitions (paras 4(3A), 4(8A)):** *date of invocation* = date borrower and AIFI agree to a Chapter VI-A resolution plan (or deemed invocation per 119N); *natural calamity* = an event recognised under **NDRF/SDRF**.
- **Board policy (para 12A):** must include objective principles for relief terms, potential relief measures + verifiable parameters, and a delegation matrix for timely implementation.
- **New Chapter VI-A – Resolution of Accounts Impacted by Calamities:**
  - **Scope (119A–119B):** applies on Government declaration of a calamity (incl. riots/disturbances causing economic loss); not to accounts already given relief before the effective date (fresh resolution after that follows this Chapter).
  - **SLBC/UTLBC/DCC role (119C–119H):** special meeting within **15 days** of declaration; assess severity, set objective criteria for impacted borrowers and any moratorium; publicise decisions.
  - **Eligibility (119J):** accounts classified **'Standard'** and **not in default > 30 days** as on the date of the calamity's occurrence. Not for the refinance portfolio (119L).
  - **Invocation/implementation (119M–119O):** invoke within **45 days**, implement within **135 days** of declaration; **deemed (suo-moto) invocation** allowed with a borrower opt-out until day 135; one-time **30-day extension** possible via RBI Regional Director.
  - **Nature (119P–119Q):** rescheduling, conversion of accrued interest into another facility, and possible additional finance — all subject to viability assessment.
  - **Ancillary (119R–119U):** factor in insurance proceeds; pass on Government interest subvention/incentives; for agricultural loans, accept **Revenue Department certificates** where original land title is lost (community certificates in Sixth-Schedule areas).
  - **Reporting (119V–119X):** upload calamity notifications to CIMS within 15 days; half-yearly relief data within 30 days of half-year end; NIL statement if none.
- **Commencement:** **July 1, 2026** (with coordinated amendments to Credit Risk Management, IRACP and Responsible Business Conduct of the same date).

_Verify with the compliance team before acting._
what_changed:
### What Changed
*(Amendment: RBI/2026-27/72, dated 2026-04-29 · effective 2026-07-01)*

| Provision | Change type | Effect |
|---|---|---|
| **Paras 4(3A), 4(8A)** | **Inserted** | Defines 'date of invocation' and 'natural calamity' (NDRF/SDRF). |
| **Para 12A** | **Inserted** | Board policy must cover calamity-resolution principles, measures and delegation. |
| **Chapter VI-A (paras 119A–119X)** | **Inserted** | Full calamity-resolution framework: SLBC/DCC role, eligibility (Standard, ≤30 days default at calamity date), 45-day invocation / 135-day implementation, deemed invocation with opt-out, relief measures, agricultural-title relaxation, CIMS reporting. |

- Consequential amendments to Credit Risk Mgmt, IRACP and Responsible Business Conduct (same date).
- **Effective date:** July 1, 2026.

_Verify with the compliance team before acting._
implications:
**Why it matters for NaBFID:** This gives NaBFID a clear, time-bound playbook to support infrastructure borrowers whose projects are disrupted by floods, cyclones or similar declared calamities — including the ability to act suo-moto (deemed invocation) without waiting for each borrower's request, provided the account was Standard and ≤30 days overdue when the calamity struck.

*Illustrative example:* After a cyclone is officially declared in a coastal district hosting a NaBFID-financed port project, NaBFID can implement a resolution plan (rescheduling + additional finance) within 135 days, keeping the account 'Standard', while reporting the relief on the CIMS portal.

_Verify with the compliance team before acting._
